Question: Find the average value of the function $$f ( x ) = \frac{x}{2}$$ over the interval $$[ 0 , 6 ]$$ and find c such that $$f ( c )$$ equals the average value of the function over $$[ 0 , 6 ] .$$
Given $$\int_{0}^{3} ( 2 x^{2} - 1 ) d x = 15 ,$$ find c such that $$f ( c )$$ equals the average value of $$f ( x ) = 2 x^{2} - 1$$ over $$[ 0 , 3 ] .$$
Use the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, Part 1 to find the derivative of $$g ( r ) = \int_{0}^{r} \sqrt{x^{2} + 4} d x .$$
Let $$F ( x ) = \int_{1}^{x^{3}} \text{cos} t d t .$$ Find $$F^{\prime} \text{(} x ) .$$
Let $$F ( x ) = \int_{x}^{x^{2}} \text{cos} t d t .$$ Find $$F^{\prime} \text{(} x ) .$$
Use The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, Part 2 to evaluate $$\int_{1}^{2} x^{-4} d x .$$
Suppose James and Kathy have a rematch, but this time the official stops the contest after only 3 sec. Does this change the outcome?
Answer: Kathy still wins, but by a much larger margin: James skates 24 ft in 3 sec, but Kathy skates 29.3634 ft in 3 sec.
